ground_truth.title: How High-Impact Tutoring Is Reshaping Post-Pandemic Learning Recovery
ground_truth.author: Liz Cohen
ground_truth.main_content: Harvard Ed Press author Liz Cohen discusses one of the most promising strategies for helping students recover from pandemic learning loss. The article explores how high-impact tutoring programs are being implemented across schools to address educational gaps created by the COVID-19 pandemic. These tutoring approaches involve one-on-one or small group instruction designed to provide targeted academic support. Schools and districts are increasingly adopting evidence-based tutoring models that show significant promise in accelerating student learning. The strategy focuses on personalized attention and intensive intervention to help struggling students catch up.
